summary: A solid matrix (AUD/SBD) recording captures a strong mid‑1974 show highlighted by an exceptional Greatest Story Ever Told and a blazing China>Rider sequence.
review: The audience‑centric recording is clear with good instrument balance, only occasional reel‑quiver noise. Musically the band delivers a consistently high‑energy set – the opening Promised Land, a fiery Scarlet Begonias, a monster Greatest Story Ever Told and a standout China Cat > I Know You Rider all shine, while the second half builds through Wharf Rat, Eyes of the World and a jam‑filled Sugar Magnolia. Minor dips appear on Ship of Fools and Big River, but overall the performance rates an 8/10.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers give 4‑star scores and praise the show’s energy and the strong audience recording; minor criticisms are limited to a few songs, supporting a rating just above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Predominantly audience (AUD) source with a brief SBD segment; solid levels and clear vocals, a handful of reel‑quiver artifacts but nothing detrimental to listening.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely from a tight Promised Land into high‑octane Scarlet Begonias and a powerful China>Rider close. The second set hits its stride with play‑in‑the‑dark improvisations, smooth segues into Wharf Rat and Eyes, then a brief tuning pause before a jam‑filled Sugar Magnolia finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His tone is praised as delightfully dirty on Me & My Uncle; locks in tightly with Phil and Bob; delivers strong solos throughout, especially on Around and Around.
band_member_comments.Phil: Locks in well with Jerry early in the show; announces a necessary tuning break before China Doll.
band_member_comments.Bob: Hammering rhythm on Around and Around; solid playing throughout.
